mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases
additional_notes: Also marketed as Xospata; active in FLT3‑ITD AML such as MOLM13; recommended top conc 1 µM for 10‑pt 3× series.

mechanism: Potent FLT3 inhibitor, selective for FLT3‑ITD
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; text notes optional top conc 1 µM for extended range but primary recommendation 100 nM.

mechanism: Pan‑FLT3 inhibition (active vs ITD and some TKD mutants)
additional_notes: Recommended top conc 1 µM; useful for FLT3‑ITD MOLM13 testing.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 (and PKC family)
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; recommended top conc 10 µM; broader kinase profile.

mechanism: Inhibits RAF, VEGFR, FLT3 and other kinases
additional_notes: Has activity against FLT3‑ITD; recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibitor with off‑target FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Heterogeneous effects in AML; recommended top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 antagonist promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Also ABT‑199; clinically active in AML in combinations (e.g., with HMAs); recommended top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits BCL‑2 and BCL‑xL (BH3 mimetic) to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; useful comparator to venetoclax; top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits XPO1 (CRM1) to block nuclear export of tumor suppressors
additional_notes: XPO1 inhibitor active in AML preclinical models; recommended top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9) affecting transcriptional regulation
additional_notes: Also called Flavopiridol; targets transcriptional addiction in AML; recommended top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Selective inhibition of CDK4 and CDK6 to induce cell‑cycle arrest
additional_notes: May affect AML proliferation in subsets; recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Pan‑histone deacetylase inhibitor altering transcription/chromatin
additional_notes: Also LBH589; active in AML models and combinations; top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits 20S proteasome leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Proteasome inhibition can be cytotoxic in AML; recommended top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA/RNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases (DNMTs)
additional_notes: Metabolically labile — may require repeat dosing/longer exposure (96 h); recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: DNMT inhibitor via incorporation into DNA and covalent trapping of DNMTs
additional_notes: Similar notes to azacitidine regarding dosing/exposure; top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA causing chain termination and inhibits DNA synthesis
additional_notes: Also called Ara‑C; standard AML chemotherapy — may require repeat dosing or 96 h exposure; recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II causing DNA damage
additional_notes: Standard anthracycline in AML regimens; recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Covalent inhibitor of BTK and additional kinases
additional_notes: Some AML subtypes show sensitivity; useful for testing combinations; recommended top conc 10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits BET family (BRD2/3/4) to disrupt oncogenic transcription programs
additional_notes: Also known as MK‑8628; investigational BET inhibitor active in AML preclinical models; top conc 1 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits MDM2 to activate p53 pathway (p53‑dependent apoptosis)
additional_notes: Also RG7388; effective only in TP53 wild‑type AML — verify MOLM13 p53 status; recommended top conc 10 µM.
